title: Sarah J Maas Expands Her Romantasy Empire with New ACOTAR Novels
summary: Sarah J Maas, a renowned romantasy author with over 75 million books sold globally, has revealed plans for two new novels in her A Court of Thorns and Roses (ACOTAR) series. The announcement was made during an interview on Alex Cooper's Call Her Daddy podcast, where Maas described the upcoming story as "really, really big" and structured across four parts within three books: ACOTAR 6, ACOTAR 7, and an unannounced fourth book. She emphasized that this narrative should be viewed as a single massive tale rather than a traditional trilogy, with arcs left unwrapped to maintain continuity. The books are scheduled for release in October of this year and January 2027, marking her first publications since 2021—her longest hiatus between novels. Maas is credited with boosting the romantasy genre's popularity, largely through discussions on #BookTok, TikTok's literary community. Her influence extends to other series like Throne of Glass and Crescent City, and her work gained significant traction during the pandemic. This move to publish two books closely together is unusual for her, highlighting the scale of the new project.

She only writes one book at a time
questions.correct_answer: It's her longest gap between novels

background_read: Sarah J Maas is a pivotal figure in modern publishing, known for creating the romantasy genre that combines epic fantasy with romantic plots. She debuted in 2015 and saw her popularity surge during the COVID-19 pandemic, as readers turned to escapist literature. Her ACOTAR series, along with Throne of Glass and Crescent City, has been translated into 40 languages, reflecting her global appeal. The rise of #BookTok on TikTok has played a crucial role in amplifying her work, with fans sharing reviews and fan art, driving sales and cultural relevance. This announcement comes after a three-year break, indicating a significant creative endeavor that may reshape her storytelling approach, moving away from traditional trilogies to more expansive narratives.
Article_Structure: Main Points: Sarah J Maas announces two new ACOTAR books, structured as a four-part story across three volumes, with releases in October and January 2027. Purpose: To inform fans and the public about her upcoming projects and highlight her influence on the romantasy genre. Evidence Evaluation: The article cites direct quotes from Maas on a podcast and sales figures, providing reliable sources, but lacks independent verification. Author Credibility: Maas is a best-selling author with proven success, though the article is from BBC Entertainment, a reputable news outlet. Methodology: Information is gathered from podcast interviews and public announcements, typical for entertainment news.

perspectives.description: Maas's work shapes genre trends and reader communities
